1.description: Economic growth and market capitalization of international markets. In this figure, Panel A plots the proportion of world GDP attributable to developed and emerging markets, while Panel B shows the share of world market capitalization attributable to emerging markets. GDP data are from the International Monetary Fund, and market capitalization data are from the World Bank. The developed markets are Australia, Canada, France, Germany, Japan, the United Kingdom and the United States. The emerging markets are Argentina, Brazil, Chile, China, Colombia, the Czech Republic, Egypt, Hungary, India, Indonesia, Malaysia, Mexico, Morocco, Pakistan, Peru, the Philippines, Poland, South Korea, Thailand, and Turkey. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Average DCC model correlations for market indices. This figure plots average correlations for developed market MSCI indices (MSCI DM), emerging market-mimicking portfolios (EM-mimicking), and emerging economy indices (EE). Correlations are computed using the dynamic conditional correlation (DCC) model. In Panel A, bilateral correlations are computed for the three sets of indices separately. For example, the black line represents the average of all bilateral correlations of EE indices. Panel B plots averages of all correlations of developed market MSCI indices with either EM-mimicking portfolios or EE indices. The countries are listed in Figure1. Construction of the EE indices and EM-mimicking portfolios is described in SectionsI.BandI.C, respectively. DCC computations do not allow for missing data, and hence the sample of correlations spans the period 1994 to 2014. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Average pairwise threshold correlations. This figure plots average pairwise threshold correlations, computed on returns that have been standardized by their unconditional means and GARCH(1,1) standard deviations. Pairwise threshold correlations are given by estimating the correlation of returns of two countries when both are below (from 0 to 0.5) or above (from 0.5 to 1) the percentile level shown on thex-axis. The dashed blue line represents the average pairwise threshold correlation across developed market MSCI indices. The dotted red line shows the corresponding values for emerging market-mimicking portfolios (EM-mimicking), and the solid black line shows the correlations for emerging economy (EE) indices. The countries are listed in Figure1. Construction of the EE indices and EM-mimicking portfolios is described in SectionsI.BandI.C, respectively. The returns span the period 1991 to 2014. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Moments of returns of country portfolios. This figure plots means, volatilities, and Sharpe ratios of returns for an equal-weighted portfolio of MSCI indices of developed markets (first bar, labeled DM), for an equal-weighted portfolio of emerging country indices (second set of bars, labeled EM), and for individual emerging countries (the remaining 20 sets of bars). For a given emerging country, results based on two different indices are shown separately: those based on emerging market-mimicking portfolios (EM-mimicking, red bars) and those based emerging economy indices (EE, black bars). Construction of the EE indices and EM-mimicking portfolios is described in SectionsI.BandI.C, respectively. The countries are listed in Figure1. Means and volatilities are in percent per year. The returns span the period 1991 to 2014. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Cumulative returns and drawdowns of market indices. This figure plots cumulative returns and drawdowns, in decimals, of the portfolio of MSCI indices of developed markets (MSCI DM), of the emerging market-mimicking portfolios (EM-mimicking), and of the emerging economy indices (EE). Portfolios are rebalanced to equal weights at the beginning of each year. Construction of the EE indices and EM-mimicking portfolios is described in SectionsI.BandI.C, respectively. The countries are listed in Figure1. The returns span the period 1991 to 2014. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Average DCC model correlations for market indices: Evidence from firm-level data. This figure plots average correlations for developed market MSCI indices (MSCI DM), emerging market-mimicking portfolios (EM-mimicking), and emerging economy indices constructed using firm-level data (EE-firm). Correlations are computed using the dynamic conditional correlation (DCC) model. In Panel A, bilateral correlations are computed for the three sets of indices separately. For example, the solid line represents the average of all bilateral correlations of EE-firm indices. Panel B plots averages of all correlations of developed market MSCI indices with either EM-mimicking portfolios or EE indices. The countries are listed in Figure1. The construction of EE-firm indices and EM-mimicking portfolios is described in SectionIIIand SectionI.C, respectively. The sample of correlations spans the period 2004 to 2014. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Moments of returns of country portfolios: Evidence from firm-level data. This figure plots means, volatilities, and Sharpe ratios of returns for MSCI USA index (first bar, labeled USA), for an equal-weighted portfolio of indices of emerging countries (second set of bars, labeled EM), and for individual emerging countries (the remaining 20 sets of bars). For a given emerging country, results based on two different indices are shown separately: those based on emerging market-mimicking portfolios (EM-mimicking, red bars) and those based emerging economy indices constructed using firm-level data (EE-firm, black bars). The countries are listed in Figure1. The construction of EE-firm indices and EM-mimicking portfolios is described in SectionIIIand SectionI.C, respectively. Means and volatilities are in percent per year. The returns span the period 2004 to 2014. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
